narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. US geopolitical de-escalation because US-Iran peace deal hopes are driving a broad dollar unwind, collapsing DXY 0.65% and anchoring the entire risk-on move across assets.
2. Safe haven unwinding because JPY crosses are selling off as the specific fear premium that had accumulated unwinds, with USDJPY, EURJPY, and GBPJPY all generating H4 sell signals against a backdrop of falling VIX.
3. Commodity currency outperformance because AUD leads D1 CSM at 100 and copper is up 4% with S&P up 0.81%, confirming genuine risk appetite rather than a simple dollar story.

PRICE CONFIRMS

The 0.94 EURUSD-GBPUSD correlation means both pairs are moving in lockstep against the dollar, and neither is leading the other, confirming this is a dollar-driven move rather than idiosyncratic currency strength. NZDUSD carries the strongest H4 signal at plus 6 and NZD tops both the D1 and H4 CSM rankings, making it the cleanest expression of USD weakness with commodity tailwinds. The JPY cross cluster, CADJPY at minus 4, USDJPY at minus 4, and EURJPY at minus 4, all confirm the safe haven unwind is broad and not pair-specific.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed. VIX is down 5.64% to 16.4, S&P 500 is up 0.81% to 7259, and the only contradiction is gold up 3.33%, which is better explained by dollar weakness and the USD-gold inverse correlation than by residual fear, making it a noise signal in this context.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold surge versus Risk-On: gold up 3.33% conflicts with pure risk-on framing, suggesting USD weakness rather than fear is the mechanism, but the signal remains ambiguous at the margin.
2. EUR H4 CSM acceleration to 95 versus D1 CSM at only 6: EUR is surging on the H4 timeframe but the daily trend structure has not confirmed, creating a timeframe conflict that limits EURUSD conviction relative to NZDUSD.

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: NZDUSD up - NZD tops all CSM rankings, H4 strong buy signal, DXY collapsing, clean alignment - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: CADJPY down - strong sell signal, CAD weakest currency, JPY safe haven unwind already captured by correlation cluster - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD - AUD CSM fading on H4 versus D1 divergence creates conflicting timeframe signals with no clean entry
Watch: DXY breaking and holding below 97.50 confirms primary NZDUSD trade; a DXY reversal back above 98.20 invalidates the entire dollar unwind thesis.

themes.theme: US-Iran Peace Deal Hopes Dominating Risk Sentiment
Safe Haven Unwinding - JPY Under Pressure Despite Intervention
Commodity Currency Strength on Risk-On Sentiment
Oil Price Collapse on Middle East De-escalation
Precious Metals Rally on USD Weakness
European FX Mixed - Political Risk in Romania, Hawkish Central Bank Bias
Risk Asset Rally - Equities and Carry Trade Support

key_observation: US-Iran peace deal prospects are the dominant driver, triggering broad USD weakness below 98.00 on the DXY, safe-haven unwinding in JPY despite BOJ intervention attempts, and strong commodity currency performance. Risk appetite has sharply reversed from geopolitical concerns to de-escalation optimism, benefiting equities and carry trades.
watch: ADP employment data release for April private sector hiring; BOJ intervention sustainability against risk-on flows; Trump ultimatum credibility and Iran response; Oil price floor establishment; EUR/JPY cross strength confirmation; commodity currency momentum continuation
